info.title: Flashduty Open API
info.description: Public HTTP API for the Flashduty incident management platform — incidents, notification templates, channels, schedules, monitors, RUM, and platform administration. Every operation is authenticated with an `app_key` query parameter issued from the Flashduty console under Account → APP Keys. Responses follow a uniform envelope: `{ request_id, data }` on success, `{ request_id, error }` on failure.

x-mint.content:
## Restrictions

| Aspect | Value |
| ------ | ----- |
| Rate limits | **1,000 requests/minute**; **50 requests/second** per account |
| Permissions | **Integrations Read** (`on-call`) or **Integrations Manage** (`on-call`) or **Mappings Read** (`on-call`) or **Mappings Manage** (`on-call`). Missing the permission → `403 AccessDenied` |

## Usage

- If `query` is provided, all source labels must be specified — partial source label queries are rejected.
- Pagination uses cursor-based (`search_after_ctx`) or page-based (`p`, `limit`) navigation. `limit` defaults to 20, max 100.
- The `search_after_ctx` token from a response can be passed back to retrieve the next page.

post.description: Set the alert processing pipeline for an integration. Replaces the existing configuration entirely.

x-mint.content:
## Restrictions

| Aspect | Value |
| ------ | ----- |
| Rate limits | **1,000 requests/minute**; **50 requests/second** per account |
| Permissions | **Integrations Manage** (`on-call`) |

## Usage

- Maximum 50 rules per pipeline.
- Each rule has a `kind` (one of `title_reset`, `description_reset`, `severity_reset`, `alert_drop`, `alert_inhibit`), an optional `if` filter, and `settings` specific to the kind.
- The `alert_inhibit` kind requires the Standard license or higher.
- Every call is recorded in the account audit log. Don't put secrets in request fields.

x-mint.content:

post.description: Create a new mapping schema defining source lookup labels and the result labels to populate. Requires a Pro plan.

x-mint.content:
## Restrictions

| Aspect | Value |
| ------ | ----- |
| Rate limits | **1,000 requests/minute**; **50 requests/second** per account |
| Permissions | **Mappings Manage** (`on-call`). Missing the permission → `403 AccessDenied` |

## Usage

- Schema names must be unique within an account.
- `source_labels` (1–3 labels) are used as lookup keys; `result_labels` (1–10 labels) are the labels written on match.
- Label names must match `^[a-z][a-z0-9_]{0,39}$` (lowercase).
- `source_labels` and `result_labels` must not overlap.
- An account can have at most 20 mapping schemas.
- Every call is recorded in the account audit log. Don't put secrets in request fields.

post.description: Create or fully replace the enrichment rule set for an integration. The entire `rules` array is replaced atomically.

x-mint.content:
## Restrictions

| Aspect | Value |
| ------ | ----- |
| Rate limits | **1,000 requests/minute**; **50 requests/second** per account |
| Permissions | **Channels Manage** (`on-call`) or **Integrations Manage** (`on-call`). Missing the permission → `403 AccessDenied` |

## Usage

- Enrichment rules are evaluated in order.
- Each rule has a `kind`: `extraction` (regex/gjson extraction), `composition` (template-based label composition), `mapping` (lookup via mapping schema or API), or `drop` (remove labels).
- The optional `if` field is an `AndFilters` condition: if it does not match, the rule is skipped.
- For `kind: extraction`: `source_field` must be `title`, `description`, or a `labels.*` key; specify exactly one of `pattern` (regex with named group `result`) or `g_json` (GJson path).
- For `kind: composition`: `template` uses Go text/template syntax referencing `labels.*` keys.
- For `kind: mapping`: `mapping_type` is `schema` (default) or `api`; provide `schema_id` or `api_id` accordingly.
- For `kind: drop`: `drop_labels` lists the label keys to remove.
- Every call is recorded in the account audit log. Don't put secrets in request fields.

## Usage

Flashduty error code enum. Every failed API response sets `error.code` to one of these stable wire strings. HTTP status is informational — the authoritative signal is the enum value.

| Code | HTTP | Meaning |
|---|---|---|
| `OK` | 200 | Reserved — not returned on real errors. |
| `InvalidParameter` | 400 | A required parameter is missing or failed validation. |
| `BadRequest` | 400 | Generic 400 used when no more specific code fits. |
| `InvalidContentType` | 400 | The `Content-Type` header is not `application/json`. |
| `ResourceNotFound` | 400 | The referenced resource does not exist. Note: returned as HTTP 400, not 404 (historical choice). |
| `NoLicense` | 400 | The feature is license-gated and no active license was found. |
| `ReferenceExist` | 400 | Deletion blocked — other entities still reference this resource. |
| `Unauthorized` | 401 | `app_key` is missing, invalid, or expired. |
| `BalanceNotEnough` | 402 | Billing-gated operation with insufficient account balance. |
| `AccessDenied` | 403 | Authenticated but lacking the permission required for this operation. |
| `RouteNotFound` | 404 | The request URL path is not a known route. |
| `MethodNotAllowed` | 405 | The HTTP method is not allowed on this otherwise-known path. |
| `UndonedOrderExist` | 409 | An outstanding billing order blocks this new one. Wait and retry. |
| `RequestLocked` | 423 | Operation temporarily locked due to repeated failures. |
| `EntityTooLarge` | 413 | Request body exceeds the configured max size. |
| `RequestTooFrequently` | 429 | Rate limit hit — API-global, per-account, or per-integration. |
| `RequestVerifyRequired` | 428 | Second-factor verification required but not supplied. |
| `DangerousOperation` | 428 | High-risk operation requires MFA verification. |
| `InternalError` | 500 | Unhandled server-side error. Include `request_id` in the bug report. |
| `ServiceUnavailable` | 503 | A backend dependency is unavailable. Try again later. |

IncidentFeedType.description:
Incident timeline entry type. Each value identifies one lifecycle event; the matching `detail` payload shape is determined by this field. Incident types are prefixed with `i_`.

| Type | Meaning |
|---|---|
| `i_new` | Incident Created: A new incident was created automatically or manually. |
| `i_assign` | Assigned: Incident was assigned to responders. |
| `i_a_rspd` | Responder Added: Additional responders joined the incident. |
| `i_notify` | Notification dispatched through a channel at a specific escalation level. |
| `i_storm` | Alert storm threshold reached on the incident. |
| `i_snooze` | Notifications snoozed for a given duration. |
| `i_wake` | Snooze cancelled and notifications resumed. |
| `i_ack` | Acknowledged: Responder confirmed they are working on the incident. |
| `i_unack` | Acknowledgement removed. |
| `i_comm` | Comment: Responder logged progress or key information. |
| `i_rslv` | Resolved: Incident was marked as resolved. |
| `i_reopen` | Reopened: Resolved incident was reopened, possibly due to recurrence. |
| `i_merge` | Merged: Multiple related incidents were merged into one. |
| `i_r_title` | Title updated. |
| `i_r_desc` | Description updated. |
| `i_r_impact` | Impact updated. |
| `i_r_rc` | Root cause updated. |
| `i_r_rsltn` | Resolution updated. |
| `i_r_severity` | Severity Changed: Incident severity level was adjusted. |
| `i_r_field` | Custom field value updated. |
| `i_m_flapping` | Incident muted by flapping detection. |
| `i_m_reply` | Mute reply marker on a comment. |
| `i_custom` | Action: Automated action or script was triggered. |
| `i_wr_create` | War Room Created: Chat group was created for collaborative response. |
| `i_wr_delete` | War room chat group deleted. |
| `i_auto_refresh` | Card auto-refresh event posted back to the timeline. |
| `a_merge` | Alert Merged: An alert was merged into an existing incident. |

version.description: Monotonic version number, incremented on each update. Use it for optimistic concurrency control.

vals.description: Values to compare against. Each value may be a literal string, a wildcard (`*`, `?`), a regular expression wrapped in slashes (`/pattern/`), a CIDR (`cidr:10.0.0.0/8`), or a numeric comparison (`num:lt:100`).

ScheduleInfoRequest.description: Schedule detail request. start/end define the window for computed layers; the span must be less than 45 days.

ScheduleItem.description: Full schedule detail returned by info/preview/list.

id.description: Schedule ID. null when returned from /schedule/preview.

name.description: Schedule name (legacy field; mirrors schedule_name). null when returned from /schedule/preview.

group_id.description: Legacy team/group ID. null when returned from /schedule/preview.

disabled.description: Disabled flag (0 = enabled, 1 = disabled). Deprecated. null when returned from /schedule/preview.

layers.description: Rotation layers defined on the schedule.

field.description: Field name used by the legacy update-field endpoint.

schedule_layers.description: Computed layers for the requested window.

final_schedule.description: Collapsed final schedule across all layers.

schedule_name.description: Schedule display name. null when returned from /schedule/preview.

team_id.description: Owning team ID. null when returned from /schedule/preview.

description.description: Schedule description. null when returned from /schedule/preview.

layer_schedules.description: Alias of schedule_layers returned for compatibility.

status.description: Legacy status flag. Deprecated. null when returned from /schedule/preview.

cur_oncall.description: Current on-call group, or null when nobody is on-call.

next_oncall.description: Next on-call group, or null when unknown.

ScheduleLayer.description: One rotation layer inside a schedule.

hidden.description: Whether the layer is hidden in the UI (0 = no, 1 = yes).

groups.description: Oncall groups participating in the rotation.

handoff_time.description: Handoff time inside the rotation cycle (seconds).

enable_time.description: When the layer becomes effective (Unix seconds).

expire_time.description: When the layer expires (Unix seconds, 0 means never).

restrict_start.description: Legacy start offset inside the restriction window (seconds).

restrict_end.description: Legacy end offset inside the restriction window (seconds).

restrict_periods.description: Restriction windows inside each rotation cycle.

layer_end.description: Layer end timestamp (Unix seconds). null means open-ended.

rotation_value.description: Rotation quantity (number of rotation_unit per cycle).

ScheduleListRequest.description: Schedule list request. limit defaults to 10 and is capped at 100; p defaults to 1. is_my_team and is_my_manage are mutually exclusive.

query.description: Search keyword matched against schedule names.

is_my_team.description: Only return schedules whose owning team the current user belongs to.

is_my_manage.description: Only return schedules created by the current user within their teams.

start.description: When set together with end, computed layer schedules are returned. Span must be less than 45 days.

total.description: Total number of schedules matching the filters.

ScheduleNotify.description: Notification configuration attached to a schedule.

follow_preference.description: Whether to follow each responder's personal notification preference.

ScheduleOncallGroup.description: Snapshot of the currently or next on-call group.

weight.description: Layer weight the shift comes from.

ScheduleRestrictPeriod.description: Restrict window inside a rotation cycle.

restrict_start.description: Start offset inside the rotation cycle.

restrict_end.description: End offset inside the rotation cycle.

ScheduleSelfRequest.description: Query parameters for listing the current user's schedules. Both start and end are Unix timestamps (seconds). If omitted they default to 0. The window must not exceed 30 days.

end.description: Window end (Unix seconds, 10 digits). Must be within 30 days of start.

ScheduleSelfResponse.description: Response of /schedule/self and /schedule/infos. Only the items field is populated.

items.description: Schedules assigned to the current user (or matching the requested IDs).

ScheduleUpsertRequest.description: Schedule create/update/preview request body. The server accepts the same shape as the schedule detail model; only the key fields are listed here. For update, set schedule_id. For preview, start and end are required.

name.description: Legacy schedule name field. Used when schedule_name is empty.

start.description: Preview window start (Unix seconds, 10 digits). Required for /schedule/preview.

end.description: Preview window end (Unix seconds, 10 digits). Required for /schedule/preview. Max 45 days after start.

from_incident_id.description: Source incident ID when the silence was created from an incident.

is_directly_discard.description: When true, silenced alerts are dropped instead of suppressed into incidents.

is_effective.description: Whether the rule is currently in effect.

SnoozeIncidentRequest.description: Parameters for snoozing notifications on one or more incidents.

incident_ids.description: Incident IDs to snooze. At most 100 per call.

minutes.description: Duration in minutes. Must be greater than 0 and at most 1440 (24h).

StatusPageChangeCreateResponse.description: Result of creating a status page event.

StatusPageChangeItem.description: A status page event (incident or maintenance).

status.description: Current event status. Incident statuses: `investigating`/`identified`/`monitoring`/`resolved`. Maintenance statuses: `scheduled`/`ongoing`/`completed`.

affected_components.description: Components currently affected by this event, with their resulting status.

responder_ids.description: Member IDs responsible for this event.

linked_change_ids.description: Linked event IDs (related incidents, deployments, etc.).

start_at_seconds.description: Event start time in unix seconds.

close_at_seconds.description: Scheduled close time in unix seconds. Set for retrospective and maintenance events.

is_retrospective.description: Whether this event is a retrospective (historical) one.

updates.description: Timeline updates attached to this event, ordered by time.

notify_subscribers.description: Whether subscribers were notified about this event.

auto_update_by_schedule.description: Maintenance only: whether the status advances automatically based on the scheduled window.

StatusPageChangeTimelineCreateResponse.description: Result of appending a timeline update to a status page event.

StatusPageChangeUpdateItem.description: A single timeline update on a status page event.

status.description: Event status after this update. Omitted when the update does not change the overall status.

component_changes.description: Component status transitions applied by this update.

StatusPageComponentChangeItem.description: Component status transition applied in a timeline update.

component_name.description: Component display name. Populated by the backend on read; ignored on write.

status.description: New component status. Incidents support `operational`/`degraded`/`partial_outage`/`full_outage`; maintenances support `operational`/`under_maintenance`.

available_since_seconds.description: Timestamp when the component was first available, in unix seconds.

hide_uptime.description: When true, uptime data is hidden from summary responses.

hide_all.description: When true, the component is hidden entirely from summary endpoints.

StatusPageMigrationJob.description: A migration job's current state and progress.

target_page_id.description: Flashduty target status page ID. Set once the job produces one, or supplied up front for subscriber migration.

updated_at.description: Last status update time, unix seconds.

StatusPageMigrationProgress.description: Progress counters for a migration job.

total_steps.description: Total steps this job will perform.

subscribers_skipped.description: Number of subscribers skipped (e.g. because they would create duplicates).

warnings.description: Non-fatal warnings recorded during the job.

StatusPageMigrationStartResponse.description: Result of starting a migration job.

job_id.description: Migration job ID. Use this to poll status or request cancellation.

StatusPageSubscriberExportResponse.description: A CSV file (Content-Type: text/csv; charset=utf-8) containing columns: Method, Recipient, Components, Subscribe All, Locale.

StatusPageSubscriberListResponse.description: Paginated list of status page subscribers.

has_next_page.description: Whether there is at least one more page after the current one.

SuccessEnvelope.description: Success response envelope. On every 2xx response, `request_id` identifies the call (also mirrored in the `Flashcat-Request-Id` header) and `data` holds the endpoint-specific payload. Failure responses use a different shape — see `ErrorResponse`.

request_id.description: Unique ID for this request. Mirrored in the Flashcat-Request-Id response header. Include it when reporting issues.

data.description: Endpoint-specific payload. See each operation's 200 response schema.

template_name.description: Template name, unique per account. 1–39 characters.

teams_app.description: Microsoft Teams app message template source.

template_name.description: Template name echoed from the request.

template_id.description: Target template ID. Pass `000000000000000000000001` to address the built-in preset.

TemplateItem.description: A notification template. Each channel field holds the template source string for that delivery channel; an empty string means 'no custom template for that channel'.

team_id.description: ID of the team this template is scoped to, or 0 for account-wide.

template_name.description: Unique template name within the account.

teams_app.description: Microsoft Teams app message template source.

updated_by.description: Member ID of the last editor.

deleted_at.description: Unix epoch seconds the template was soft-deleted. Absent (omitempty) when the template is live.

created_at.description: Unix epoch seconds the template was created.

updated_at.description: Unix epoch seconds the template was last updated.

TemplateListRequest.description: Paginated list filters. Defaults: p=1, limit=20. Max limit=100.

is_my_team.description: When true, only return templates scoped to teams the caller belongs to.

query.description: Regex or substring match on template_name.

total.description: Total number of templates matching the filter, across all pages.

has_next_page.description: True if another page exists after the returned one.

teams_app.description: Microsoft Teams app message template source.

TimeFilter.description: Recurring time window. `start`/`end` use 24-hour `HH:MM` format; `repeat` lists ISO-style weekday indices (0=Sunday … 6=Saturday).

repeat.description: Days of the week this window repeats on. Empty means every day.

cal_id.description: Optional calendar ID; restricts the window to days matching the calendar.

is_off.description: When true, match days marked as days-off in the calendar.

UnackIncidentRequest.description: Parameters for removing acknowledgement from one or more incidents.

incident_ids.description: Incident IDs to unacknowledge. At most 100 per call.

UpdateChannelRequest.description: Parameters for updating a channel. Only the fields you pass are updated.

managing_team_ids.description: Additional teams that can manage the channel. Up to 3 entries.

auto_resolve_timeout.description: Auto-resolve timeout in seconds. 0 disables auto-resolve. Max 30 days.

is_private.description: When true, the channel is visible only to its managing teams.

is_external_report_enabled.description: Allow external reporters to file incidents into this channel.

UpdateChannelResponse.description: Response from updating a channel. Only populated when `is_external_report_enabled` is set to `true`; otherwise all fields are empty.

external_report_token.description: Newly generated token for external reporters. Only returned when `is_external_report_enabled` is set to `true` in the request. Callers should store this value; it cannot be retrieved afterwards.

UpdateDropRuleRequest.description: Parameters for updating a channel drop rule.

UpdateEscalationRuleRequest.description: Parameters for updating an escalation rule.

aggr_window.description: Aggregation window in seconds. 0 disables aggregation.

layers.description: Escalation levels in order. At least one level is required.

time_filters.description: Optional recurring time windows during which the rule applies.

UpdateIncidentFieldsRequest.description: Parameters for updating user-editable fields of an incident. At least one field must be provided; the handler will update only the fields you supply.

UpdateInhibitRuleRequest.description: Parameters for updating an inhibit rule.

equals.description: Label keys used to pair source and target alerts.

is_directly_discard.description: When true, suppressed target alerts are dropped instead of merged.

UpdateSilenceRuleRequest.description: Parameters for updating a silence rule. Exactly one of `time_filter` or `time_filters` must be provided, and `filters` must be non-empty.

time_filters.description: Recurring time windows. Mutually exclusive with `time_filter`.

is_directly_discard.description: When true, silenced alerts are dropped instead of suppressed into incidents.

UpdateStatusPageChangeRequest.description: Parameters for updating the editable fields of a status page event.

title.description: New event title, up to 255 characters. Omit to keep the existing value.

responders.description: Member IDs responsible for this event. Pass the full replacement list.

linked_changes.description: Linked event IDs. Pass the full replacement list.

UpdateStatusPageChangeTimelineRequest.description: Parameters for updating an existing timeline entry on a status page event.

at_seconds.description: New update timestamp in unix seconds.

UpsertRouteRequest.description: Parameters for creating or updating the routing rule of an integration. The handler creates a rule when none exists for the integration, otherwise it overwrites the existing one. At least one of `cases` or `default` must be provided.

integration_id.description: Integration the rule belongs to.

cases.description: Ordered list of case branches. Cases are evaluated top to bottom.

sections.description: Optional sections that group consecutive cases for display.

version.description: Expected current version for optimistic concurrency control. Pass the value returned by the latest read.

incident_ids.description: Incident IDs to wake. At most 100 per call.

WarRoom.description: Live war room details fetched from the IM side. Returns an empty object if the group was deleted externally.

chat_id.description: Chat/group ID on the IM side.

share_link.description: Join link for the war room, if provided by the IM.

integration_id.description: IM integration ID.

created_by.description: Member ID that created the war room.

chat_id.description: Chat/group ID on the IM side.

WebhookHistoryDetail.description: Full detail for a webhook delivery attempt. Extends `WebhookHistoryItem` with human-friendly reference metadata resolved at query time.

integration_id.description: Integration ID.

duration.description: Total elapsed time of the attempt in milliseconds.

event_time.description: Event time as a formatted timestamp string.

ref_title.description: Title of the source incident or alert, resolved at query time.

channel_name.description: Name of the associated channel, resolved at query time.

WebhookHistoryItem.description: A single webhook delivery attempt. Fields with `omitempty` are absent when zero-valued.

integration_id.description: Integration ID that triggered the webhook.

event_id.description: Unique event identifier for the delivery attempt.

channel_id.description: Channel ID associated with the event, when applicable.

ref_id.description: Source object ID (incident ID or alert ID).

duration.description: Total elapsed time of the attempt in milliseconds.

status_code.description: HTTP status code returned by the destination.

response_headers.description: Serialized response headers from the destination.

response_body.description: Response body returned by the destination.

event_time.description: Event time as a formatted timestamp string.
